results.name: Abacus
results.desc: An abacus.

results.name: Acid
results.desc: As an action, you can splash the contents of this vial onto a creature within 5 feet of you or throw the vial up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. In either case, make a ranged attack against a creature or object, treating the acid as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 acid damage.

results.name: Acid (vial)
results.desc: As an action, you can splash the contents of this vial onto a creature within 5 feet of you or throw the vial up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. In either case, make a ranged attack against a creature or object, treating the acid as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target takes 2d6 acid damage.

results.name: Alchemist's Fire (Flask)
results.desc: This sticky, adhesive fluid ignites when exposed to air. As an action, you can throw this flask up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. Make a ranged attack against a creature or object, treating the alchemist's fire as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target takes 1d4 fire damage at the start of each of its turns. A creature can end this damage by using its action to make a DC 10 Dexterity check to extinguish the flames.

results.name: Alchemist's Supplies
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Amulet
results.desc: Can be used as a holy symbol.

results.name: Antitoxin (Vial)
results.desc: A creature that drinks this vial of liquid gains advantage on saving throws against poison for 1 hour. It confers no benefit to undead or constructs.

results.name: Arrow (bow)
results.desc: An arrow for a bow.

results.name: Assassin's Blood
results.desc:
A creature subjected to this poison must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes 6 (1d12) poison damage and is poisoned for 24 hours. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and isn't poisoned.\n\n

**_Ingested._** A creature must swallow an entire dose of ingested poison to suffer its effects. The dose can be delivered in food or a liquid. You may decide that a partial dose has a reduced effect, such as allowing advantage on the saving throw or dealing only half damage on a failed save.

results.name: Backpack
results.desc: A backpack. Capacity: 1 cubic foot/30 pounds of gear.

results.name: Bagpipes
results.desc: Several of the most common types of musical instruments are shown on the table as examples. If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Ball Bearings (bag of 1000)
results.desc: As an action, you can spill these tiny metal balls from their pouch to cover a level, square area that is 10 feet on a side. A creature moving across the covered area must succeed on a DC 10 Dexterity saving throw or fall prone. A creature moving through the area at half speed doesn't need to make the save.

results.name: Barrel
results.desc: A barrel. Capacity: 40 gallons liquid, 4 cubic feet solid.

results.name: Basket
results.desc: A basket. Capacity: 2 cubic feet/40 pounds of gear.

results.name: Battleaxe
results.desc: A battleaxe.

property.name: Versatile
property.desc: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

results.name: Bedroll
results.desc: A bedroll.

results.name: Bell
results.desc: A bell.

results.name: Blanket
results.desc: A blanket.

results.name: Block and Tackle
results.desc: A set of pulleys with a cable threaded through them and a hook to attach to objects, a block and tackle allows you to hoist up to four times the weight you can normally lift.

results.name: Blowgun
results.desc: A blowgun.

property.name: Ammunition
property.desc:
You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-­‐‑handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.

If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see “Improvised Weapons” later in the section). A sling must be loaded to deal any damage when used in this way.

property.name: Loading
property.desc: Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

results.name: Blowgun needles
results.desc: Needles to be fired with a blowgun.

results.name: Book
results.desc: A book might contain poetry, historical accounts, information pertaining to a particular field of lore, diagrams and notes on gnomish contraptions, or just about anything else that can be represented using text or pictures. A book of spells is a spellbook (described later in this section).

results.name: Bottle, glass
results.desc: A glass bottle. Capacity: 1.5 pints liquid.

results.name: Breastplate
results.desc: This armor consists of a fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Although it leaves the legs and arms relatively unprotected, this armor provides good protection for the wearer's vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.

results.name: Brewer's Supplies
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Bucket
results.desc: A bucket. Capacity: 3 gallons liquid, 1/2 cubic foot solid.

results.name: Burnt othur fumes
results.desc: A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or take 10 (3d6) poison damage, and must repeat the saving throw at the start of each of its turns. On each successive failed save, the character takes 3 (1d6) poison damage. After three successful saves, the poison ends.\n\n**_Inhaled._** These poisons are powders or gases that take effect when inhaled. Blowing the powder or releasing the gas subjects creatures in a 5-foot cube to its effect. The resulting cloud dissipates immediately afterward. Holding one's breath is ineffective against inhaled poisons, as they affect nasal membranes, tear ducts, and other parts of the body.

results.name: Calligrapher's supplies
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Caltrops (bag of 20)
results.desc: As an action, you can spread a bag of caltrops to cover a square area that is 5 feet on a side. Any creature that enters the area must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or stop moving this turn and take 1 piercing damage. Taking this damage reduces the creature's walking speed by 10 feet until the creature regains at least 1 hit point. A creature moving through the area at half speed doesn't need to make the save.

results.name: Candle
results.desc: For 1 hour, a candle sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet.

results.name: Canvas
results.desc: 1 sq. yd. of canvas

results.name: Carpenter's Tools
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Carriage
results.desc: Drawn vehicle.

results.name: Cart
results.desc: Drawn vehicle

results.name: Cartographer's Tools
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Case, Crossbow Bolt
results.desc: This wooden case can hold up to twenty crossbow bolts.

results.name: Case, Map or Scroll
results.desc: This cylindrical leather case can hold up to ten rolled-up sheets of paper or five rolled-up sheets of parchment.

results.name: Chain (10 feet)
results.desc: A chain has 10 hit points. It can be burst with a successful DC 20 Strength check.

results.name: Chain mail
results.desc: Made of interlocking metal rings, chain mail includes a layer of quilted fabric worn underneath the mail to prevent chafing and to cushion the impact of blows. The suit includes gauntlets.

results.name: Chain shirt
results.desc: Made of interlocking metal rings, a chain shirt is worn between layers of clothing or leather. This armor offers modest protection to the wearer's upper body and allows the sound of the rings rubbing against one another to be muffled by outer layers.

results.name: Chalk (1 piece)
results.desc: A piece of chalk.

results.name: Chariot
results.desc: Drawn vehicle.

results.name: Chest
results.desc: A chest. Capacity: 12 cubic feet/300 pounds of gear.

results.name: Chicken
results.desc: One chicken

results.name: Cinnamon
results.desc: 1lb of cinnamon

results.name: Climber's Kit
results.desc: A climber's kit includes special pitons, boot tips, gloves, and a harness. You can use the climber's kit as an action to anchor yourself; when you do, you can't fall more than 25 feet from the point where you anchored yourself, and you can't climb more than 25 feet away from that point without undoing the anchor.

results.name: Clothes, Common
results.desc: Common clothes.

results.name: Clothes, costume
results.desc: A costume.

results.name: Clothes, fine
results.desc: Fine clothing.

results.name: Clothes, traveler's
results.desc: Traveler's clothing.

results.name: Cloves
results.desc: 1lb of cloves.

results.name: Club
results.desc: A club

property.name: Light
property.desc: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

results.name: Cobbler's Tools
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Component Pouch
results.desc: A component pouch is a small, watertight leather belt pouch that has compartments to hold all the material components and other special items you need to cast your spells, except for those components that have a specific cost (as indicated in a spell's description).

results.name: Cook's utensils
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Copper
results.desc: 1lb of copper.

results.name: Copper Piece
results.desc: One silver piece is worth ten copper pieces, which are common among laborers and beggars. A single copper piece buys a candle, a torch, or a piece of chalk.

results.name: Cotton Cloth
results.desc: 1 sq. yd. of cotton cloth.

results.name: Cow
results.desc: One cow.

results.name: Crawler mucus
results.desc:
This poison must be harvested from a dead or incapacitated crawler. A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 minute. The poisoned creature is paralyzed. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.\n\n
**_Contact._** Contact poison can be smeared on an object and remains potent until it is touched or washed off. A creature that touches contact poison with exposed skin suffers its effects.

results.name: Crossbow bolt
results.desc: Bolts to be used in a crossbow.

results.name: Crossbow, hand
results.desc: A hand crossbow.

property.name: Ammunition
property.desc:
You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-­‐‑handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.

If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see “Improvised Weapons” later in the section). A sling must be loaded to deal any damage when used in this way.

property.name: Light
property.desc: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

property.name: Loading
property.desc: Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

results.name: Crossbow, heavy
results.desc: A heavy crossbow

property.name: Loading
property.desc: Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

property.name: Two-Handed
property.desc: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

results.name: Crossbow, light
results.desc: A light crossbow.

property.name: Ammunition
property.desc:
You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-­‐‑handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.

If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see “Improvised Weapons” later in the section). A sling must be loaded to deal any damage when used in this way.

property.name: Loading
property.desc: Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

results.name: Crowbar
results.desc: Using a crowbar grants advantage to Strength checks where the crowbar's leverage can be applied.

results.name: Crystal
results.desc: Can be used as an arcane focus.

results.name: Dagger
results.desc: A dagger.

property.name: Finesse
property.desc: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

property.name: Light
property.desc: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

property.name: Thrown
property.desc: If a weapon has the thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

results.name: Dart
results.desc: A dart.

property.name: Finesse
property.desc: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

property.name: Thrown
property.desc: If a weapon has the thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

results.name: Dice set
results.desc: This item encompasses a wide range of game pieces, including dice and decks of cards (for games such as Three-­‐‑Dragon Ante). A few common examples appear on the Tools table, but other kinds of gaming sets exist. If you are proficient with a gaming set, you can add your proficiency bonus to ability checks you make to play a game with that set. Each type of gaming set requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Disguise kit
results.desc: This pouch of cosmetics, hair dye, and small props lets you create disguises that change your physical appearance. Proficiency with this kit lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to create a visual disguise.

results.name: Drow poison
results.desc:
This poison is typically made only by the drow, and only in a place far removed from sunlight. A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or be poisoned for 1 hour. If the saving throw fails by 5 or more, the creature is also unconscious while poisoned in this way. The creature wakes up if it takes damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake.
\n\n**_Injury._** Injury poison can be applied to weapons, ammunition, trap components, and other objects that deal piercing or slashing damage and remains potent until delivered through a wound or washed off. A creature that takes piercing or slashing damage from an object coated with the poison is exposed to its effects.

results.name: Drum
results.desc: Several of the most common types of musical instruments are shown on the table as examples. If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Dulcimer
results.desc: Several of the most common types of musical instruments are shown on the table as examples. If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Electrum Piece
results.desc: In addition, unusual coins made of other precious metals sometimes appear in treasure hoards. The electrum piece (ep) and the platinum piece (pp) originate from fallen empires and lost kingdoms, and they sometimes arouse suspicion and skepticism when used in transactions. An electrum piece is worth five silver pieces, and a platinum piece is worth ten gold pieces.

results.name: Emblem
results.desc: Can be used as a holy symbol.

results.name: Essense of either
results.desc: A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 8 hours. The poisoned creature is unconscious. The creature wakes up if it takes damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake.\n\n**_Inhaled._** These poisons are powders or gases that take effect when inhaled. Blowing the powder or releasing the gas subjects creatures in a 5-foot cube to its effect. The resulting cloud dissipates immediately afterward. Holding one's breath is ineffective against inhaled poisons, as they affect nasal membranes, tear ducts, and other parts of the body.

results.name: Fishing Tackle
results.desc: This kit includes a wooden rod, silken line, corkwood bobbers, steel hooks, lead sinkers, velvet lures, and narrow netting.

results.name: Flail
results.desc: A flail.

results.name: Flask or tankard
results.desc: For drinking. Capacity: 1 pint liquid.

results.name: Flour
results.desc: 1lb of flour

results.name: Flute
results.desc: Several of the most common types of musical instruments are shown on the table as examples. If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Forgery kit
results.desc: This small box contains a variety of papers and parchments, pens and inks, seals and sealing wax, gold and silver leaf, and other supplies necessary to create convincing forgeries of physical documents. \n\nProficiency with this kit lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to create a physical forgery of a document.

results.name: Galley
results.desc: A waterborne vehicle. Speed 4 mph

results.name: Ginger
results.desc: 1lb of Ginger.

results.name: Glaive
results.desc: A glaive.

property.name: Reach
property.desc: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

property.name: Two-Handed
property.desc: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

results.name: Glassblower's Tools
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Goat
results.desc: One goat.

results.name: Gold
results.desc: 1lb of gold.

results.name: Gold Piece
results.desc: With one gold piece, a character can buy a bedroll, 50 feet of good rope, or a goat. A skilled (but not exceptional) artisan can earn one gold piece a day. The gold piece is the standard unit of measure for wealth, even if the coin itself is not commonly used. When merchants discuss deals that involve goods or services worth hundreds or thousands of gold pieces, the transactions don't usually involve the exchange of individual coins. Rather, the gold piece is a standard measure of value, and the actual exchange is in gold bars, letters of credit, or valuable goods.

results.name: Grappling hook
results.desc: A grappling hook.

results.name: Greataxe
results.desc: A greataxe.

property.name: Two-Handed
property.desc: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

results.name: Greatclub
results.desc: A greatclub.

property.name: Two-Handed
property.desc: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

results.name: Greatsword
results.desc: A great sword.

property.name: Two-Handed
property.desc: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

results.name: Halberd
results.desc: A halberd.

property.name: Reach
property.desc: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

property.name: Two-Handed
property.desc: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

results.name: Half plate
results.desc: Half plate consists of shaped metal plates that cover most of the wearer's body. It does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves that are attached with leather straps.

results.name: Hammer
results.desc: A hammer.

results.name: Hammer, sledge
results.desc: A sledgehammer

results.name: Handaxe
results.desc: A handaxe.

property.name: Light
property.desc: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

results.name: Healer's Kit
results.desc: This kit is a leather pouch containing bandages, salves, and splints. The kit has ten uses. As an action, you can expend one use of the kit to stabilize a creature that has 0 hit points, without needing to make a Wisdom (Medicine) check.

results.name: Herbalism Kit
results.desc: This kit contains a variety of instruments such as clippers, mortar and pestle, and pouches and vials used by herbalists to create remedies and potions. Proficiency with this kit lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to identify or apply herbs. Also, proficiency with this kit is required to create antitoxin and potions of healing.

results.name: Hide Armor
results.desc: This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts. It is commonly worn by barbarian tribes, evil humanoids, and other folk who lack access to the tools and materials needed to create better armor.

results.name: Holy Water (flask)
results.desc: As an action, you can splash the contents of this flask onto a creature within 5 feet of you or throw it up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact.In either case, make a ranged attack against a target creature, treating the holy water as an improvised weapon. If the target is a fiend or undead, it takes 2d6 radiant damage.\n\nA cleric or paladin may create holy water by performing a special ritual. The ritual takes 1 hour to perform, uses 25 gp worth of powdered silver, and requires the caster to expend a 1st-­‐‑level spell slot.

results.name: Horn
results.desc: Several of the most common types of musical instruments are shown on the table as examples. If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Hourglass
results.desc: An hourglass.

results.name: Hunting Trap
results.desc: When you use your action to set it, this trap forms a saw-­‐‑toothed steel ring that snaps shut when a creature steps on a pressure plate in the center. The trap is affixed by a heavy chain to an immobile object, such as a tree or a spike driven into the ground. A creature that steps on the plate must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 1d4 piercing damage and stop moving. Thereafter, until the creature breaks free of the trap, its movement is limited by the length of the chain (typically 3 feet long). A creature can use its action to make a DC 13 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Each failed check deals 1 piercing damage to the trapped creature.

results.name: Ink (1 ounce bottle)
results.desc: A bottle of ink.

results.name: Ink pen
results.desc: A pen for writing with ink.

results.name: Iron
results.desc: 1lb of iron.

results.name: Javelin
results.desc: A javelin

property.name: Thrown
property.desc: If a weapon has the thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

results.name: Jeweler's Tools
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Jug or pitcher
results.desc: For drinking a lot. Capacity: 1 gallon liquid.

results.name: Keelboat
results.desc: Waterborne vehicle. Speed is 1mph.

results.name: Ladder (10-foot)
results.desc: A 10 foot ladder.

results.name: Lamp
results.desc: A lamp casts bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Once lit, it burns for 6 hours on a flask (1 pint) of oil.

results.name: Lamp oil (flask)
results.desc: Oil usually comes in a clay flask that holds 1 pint. As an action, you can splash the oil in this flask onto a creature within 5 feet of you or throw it up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. Make a ranged attack against a target creature or object, treating the oil as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target is covered in oil. If the target takes any fire damage before the oil dries (after 1 minute), the target takes an additional 5 fire damage from the burning oil. You can also pour a flask of oil on the ground to cover a 5-­foot‑square area, provided that the surface is level. If lit, the oil burns for 2 rounds and deals 5 fire damage to any creature that enters the area or ends its turn in the area. A creature can take this damage only once per turn.

results.name: Lance
results.desc:
A lance.

You have disadvantage when you use a lance to attack a target within 5 feet of you. Also, a lance requires two hands to wield when you aren't mounted.

property.name: Special (Lance)
property.desc: You have disadvantage when you use alance to attack a target within 5 feet of you. Also, a lance requires two hands to wield when you aren’t mounted.

property.name: Reach
property.desc: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

results.name: Lantern, Bullseye
results.desc: A bullseye lantern casts bright light in a 60-­‐‑foot cone and dim light for an additional 60 feet. Once lit, it burns for 6 hours on a flask (1 pint) of oil.

results.name: Lantern, Hooded
results.desc: A hooded lantern casts bright light in a 30-­‐‑foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Once lit, it burns for 6 hours on a flask (1 pint) of oil. As an action, you can lower the hood, reducing the light to dim light in a 5-­‐‑foot radius.

results.name: Leather Armor
results.desc: The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.

results.name: Leatherworker's Tools
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Light hammer
results.desc: A light hammer.

property.name: Light
property.desc: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

property.name: Thrown
property.desc: If a weapon has the thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

results.name: Linen
results.desc: 1 sq. yd. of linen.

results.name: Lock
results.desc: A key is provided with the lock. Without the key, a creature proficient with thieves’ tools can pick this lock with a successful DC 15 Dexterity check. Your GM may decide that better locks are available for higher prices.

results.name: Longbow
results.desc: A longbow.

property.name: Ammunition
property.desc:
You can use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have ammunition to fire from the weapon. Each time you attack with the weapon, you expend one piece of ammunition. Drawing the ammunition from a quiver, case, or other container is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load a one-­‐‑handed weapon). At the end of the battle, you can recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield.

If you use a weapon that has the ammunition property to make a melee attack, you treat the weapon as an improvised weapon (see “Improvised Weapons” later in the section). A sling must be loaded to deal any damage when used in this way.

property.name: Two-Handed
property.desc: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

results.name: Longship
results.desc: Waterborne vehicle. Speed 3mph.

results.name: Longsword
results.desc: A longsword

property.name: Versatile
property.desc: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

results.name: Lute
results.desc: Several of the most common types of musical instruments are shown on the table as examples. If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Lyre
results.desc: Several of the most common types of musical instruments are shown on the table as examples. If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Mace
results.desc: A mace.

results.name: Magnifying Glass
results.desc: This lens allows a closer look at small objects. It is also useful as a substitute for flint and steel when starting fires. Lighting a fire with a magnifying glass requires light as bright as sunlight to focus, tinder to ignite, and about 5 minutes for the fire to ignite. A magnifying glass grants advantage on any ability check made to appraise or inspect an item that is small or highly detailed.

results.name: Malice
results.desc: A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 1 hour. The poisoned creature is blinded.\n\n**_Inhaled._** These poisons are powders or gases that take effect when inhaled. Blowing the powder or releasing the gas subjects creatures in a 5-foot cube to its effect. The resulting cloud dissipates immediately afterward. Holding one's breath is ineffective against inhaled poisons, as they affect nasal membranes, tear ducts, and other parts of the body.

results.name: Manacles
results.desc: These metal restraints can bind a Small or Medium creature. Escaping the manacles requires a successful DC 20 Dexterity check. Breaking them requires a successful DC 20 Strength check. Each set of manacles comes with one key. Without the key, a creature proficient with thieves’ tools can pick the manacles’ lock with a successful DC 15 Dexterity check. Manacles have 15 hit points.

results.name: Mason's Tools
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Maul
results.desc: A maul.

property.name: Two-Handed
property.desc: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

results.name: Mess Kit
results.desc: This tin box contains a cup and simple cutlery. The box clamps together, and one side can be used as a cooking pan and the other as a plate or shallow bowl.

results.name: Midnight tears
results.desc: A creature that ingests this poison suffers no effect until the stroke of midnight. If the poison has not been neutralized before then, the creature must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, taking 31 (9d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.\n\n **_Ingested._** A creature must swallow an entire dose of ingested poison to suffer its effects. The dose can be delivered in food or a liquid. You may decide that a partial dose has a reduced effect, such as allowing advantage on the saving throw or dealing only half damage on a failed save.

results.name: Mirror, steel
results.desc: A mirror.

results.name: Morningstar
results.desc: A morningstar

results.name: Navigator's tools
results.desc: This set of instruments is used for navigation at sea. Proficiency with navigator's tools lets you chart a ship's course and follow navigation charts. In addition, these tools allow you to add your proficiency bonus to any ability check you make to avoid getting lost at sea.

results.name: Net
results.desc:
A net.

A Large or smaller creature hit by a net is restrained until it is freed. A net has no effect on creatures that are formless, or creatures that are Huge or larger. A creature can use its action to make a DC 10 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the net (AC 10) also frees the creature without harming it, ending the effect and destroying the net.

When you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to attack with a net, you can make only one attack regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

property.name: Special (Net)
property.desc:
A Large or smaller creature hit by a net is restrained until it is freed. A net has no effect on creatures that are formless, or creatures that are Huge or larger. A creature can use its action to make a DC 10 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the net (AC 10) also frees the creature without harming it, ending the effect and destroying the net.

When you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to attack with a net, you can make only one attack regardless of the number of attacks you can normally make.

property.name: Thrown
property.desc: If a weapon has the thrown property, you can throw the weapon to make a ranged attack. If the weapon is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier for that attack roll and damage roll that you would use for a melee attack with the weapon. For example, if you throw a handaxe, you use your Strength, but if you throw a dagger, you can use either your Strength or your Dexterity, since the dagger has the finesse property.

results.name: Oil of taggit
results.desc: A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 24 hours. The poisoned creature is unconscious. The creature wakes up if it takes damage.\n\n **_Contact._** Contact poison can be smeared on an object and remains potent until it is touched or washed off. A creature that touches contact poison with exposed skin suffers its effects.

results.name: Orb
results.desc: Can be used as an Arcane Focus.

results.name: Ox
results.desc: One ox.

results.name: Padded Armor
results.desc: Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.

results.name: Painter's Supplies
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Pale tincture
results.desc: A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or take 3 (1d6) poison damage and become poisoned. The poisoned creature must repeat the saving throw every 24 hours, taking 3 (1d6) poison damage on a failed save. Until this poison ends, the damage the poison deals can't be healed by any means. After seven successful saving throws, the effect ends and the creature can heal normally. \n\n **_Ingested._** A creature must swallow an entire dose of ingested poison to suffer its effects. The dose can be delivered in food or a liquid. You may decide that a partial dose has a reduced effect, such as allowing advantage on the saving throw or dealing only half damage on a failed save.

results.name: Pan flute
results.desc: Several of the most common types of musical instruments are shown on the table as examples. If you have proficiency with a given musical instrument, you can add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to play music with the instrument. A bard can use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus. Each type of musical instrument requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Paper (one sheet)
results.desc: A sheet of paper

results.name: Parchment (one sheet)
results.desc: A sheet of parchment

results.name: Pepper
results.desc: 1lb of pepper.

results.name: Perfume (vial)
results.desc: A vial of perfume.

results.name: Pick, miner's
results.desc: A pick for mining.

results.name: Pig
results.desc: One pig.

results.name: Pike
results.desc: A pike.

property.name: Reach
property.desc: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

property.name: Two-Handed
property.desc: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

results.name: Piton
results.desc: A piton for climbing.

results.name: Plate Armor
results.desc: Plate consists of shaped, interlocking metal plates to cover the entire body. A suit of plate includes gauntlets, heavy leather boots, a visored helmet, and thick layers of padding underneath the armor. Buckles and straps distribute the weight over the body.

results.name: Platinum
results.desc: 1 lb. of platinum.

results.name: Platinum Piece
results.desc: In addition, unusual coins made of other precious metals sometimes appear in treasure hoards. The electrum piece (ep) and the platinum piece (pp) originate from fallen empires and lost kingdoms, and they sometimes arouse suspicion and skepticism when used in transactions. An electrum piece is worth five silver pieces, and a platinum piece is worth ten gold pieces.

results.name: Playing card set
results.desc: This item encompasses a wide range of game pieces, including dice and decks of cards (for games such as Three-­‐‑Dragon Ante). A few common examples appear on the Tools table, but other kinds of gaming sets exist. If you are proficient with a gaming set, you can add your proficiency bonus to ability checks you make to play a game with that set. Each type of gaming set requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Poison, Basic
results.desc: You can use the poison in this vial to coat one slashing or piercing weapon or up to three pieces of ammunition. Applying the poison takes an action. A creature hit by the poisoned weapon or ammunition must make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or take 1d4 poison damage. Once applied, the poison retains potency for 1 minute before drying.

results.name: Poisoner's kit
results.desc: A poisoner’s kit includes the vials, chemicals, and other equipment necessary for the creation of poisons. Proficiency with this kit lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to craft or use poisons.

results.name: Pole (10-foot)
results.desc: A 10 foot pole.

results.name: Pot, iron
results.desc: An iron pot. Capacity: 1 gallon liquid.

results.name: Potter's tools
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Pouch
results.desc:
A cloth or leather pouch can hold up to 20 sling bullets or 50 blowgun needles, among other things. A compartmentalized pouch for holding spell components is called a component pouch (described earlier in this section).
Capacity: 1/5 cubic foot/6 pounds of gear.

results.name: Purple worm poison
results.desc:
This poison must be harvested from a dead or incapacitated purple worm. A creature subjected to this poison must make a DC 19 Constitution saving throw, taking 42 (12d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
\n\n
**_Injury._** Injury poison can be applied to weapons, ammunition, trap components, and other objects that deal piercing or slashing damage and remains potent until delivered through a wound or washed off. A creature that takes piercing or slashing damage from an object coated with the poison is exposed to its effects.

results.name: Quarterstaff
results.desc: A quarterstaff.

property.name: Versatile
property.desc: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

results.name: Quiver
results.desc: A quiver can hold up to 20 arrows.

results.name: Ram, Portable
results.desc: You can use a portable ram to break down doors. When doing so, you gain a +4 bonus on the Strength check. One other character can help you use the ram, giving you advantage on this check.

results.name: Rapier
results.desc: A rapier.

property.name: Finesse
property.desc: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

results.name: Rations (1 day)
results.desc: Rations consist of dry foods suitable for extended travel, including jerky, dried fruit, hardtack, and nuts.

results.name: Reliquary
results.desc: Can be used as a holy symbol.

results.name: Ring mail
results.desc: This armor is leather armor with heavy rings sewn into it. The rings help reinforce the armor against blows from swords and axes. Ring mail is inferior to chain mail, and it's usually worn only by those who can't afford better armor.

results.name: Robes
results.desc: Robes, for wearing.

results.name: Rod
results.desc: Can be used as an arcane focus.

results.name: Rope, hempen (50 feet)
results.desc: Rope, whether made of hemp or silk, has 2 hit points and can be burst with a DC 17 Strength check.

results.name: Rope, silk (50 feet)
results.desc: Rope, whether made of hemp or silk, has 2 hit points and can be burst with a DC 17 Strength check.

results.name: Rowboat
results.desc: Waterborne vehicle. Speed 1.5mph

results.name: Sack
results.desc: A sack. Capacity: 1 cubic foot/30 pounds of gear.

results.name: Saffron
results.desc: 1lb of saffron.

results.name: Sailing Ship
results.desc: Waterborne vehicle. Speed 2mph

results.name: Salt
results.desc: 1lb of salt.

results.name: Scale mail
results.desc: This armor consists of a coat and leggings (and perhaps a separate skirt) of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like the scales of a fish. The suit includes gauntlets.

results.name: Scale, Merchant's
results.desc: A scale includes a small balance, pans, and a suitable assortment of weights up to 2 pounds. With it, you can measure the exact weight of small objects, such as raw precious metals or trade goods, to help determine their worth.

results.name: Scimitar
results.desc: A scimitar.

property.name: Finesse
property.desc: When making an attack with a finesse weapon, you use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.

property.name: Light
property.desc: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for use when fighting with two weapons.

results.name: Sealing wax
results.desc: For sealing written letters.

results.name: Serpent venom
results.desc:
This poison must be harvested from a dead or incapacitated giant poisonous snake. A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw, taking 10 (3d6) poison damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
\n\n
**_Injury._** Injury poison can be applied to weapons, ammunition, trap components, and other objects that deal piercing or slashing damage and remains potent until delivered through a wound or washed off. A creature that takes piercing or slashing damage from an object coated with the poison is exposed to its effects.

results.name: Sheep
results.desc: One sheep.

results.name: Shield
results.desc: A shield is made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your Armor Class by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

results.name: Shortbow
results.desc: A shortbow

results.name: Silver Piece
results.desc: One gold piece is worth ten silver pieces, the most prevalent coin among commoners. A silver piece buys a laborer's work for half a day, a flask of lamp oil, or a night's rest in a poor inn.

results.name: Sled
results.desc: Drawn vehicle

results.name: Sling
results.desc: A sling.

results.name: Spellbook
results.desc: Essential for wizards, a spellbook is a leather-­‐‑bound tome with 100 blank vellum pages suitable for recording spells.

results.name: Spike, iron
results.desc: An iron spike.

results.name: Splint Armor
results.desc: This armor is made of narrow vertical strips of metal riveted to a backing of leather that is worn over cloth padding. Flexible chain mail protects the joints.

results.name: Sprig of mistletoe
results.desc: A sprig of mistletoe that can be used as a druidic focus.

results.name: Spyglass
results.desc: Objects viewed through a spyglass are magnified to twice their size.

results.name: Staff
results.desc: Can be used as an arcane focus.

property.name: Versatile
property.desc: This weapon can be used with one or two hands. A damage value in parentheses appears with the property—the damage when the weapon is used with two hands to make a melee attack.

results.name: Studded Leather Armor
results.desc: Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.

results.name: Tent
results.desc: A simple and portable canvas shelter, a tent sleeps two.

results.name: Thieves' tools
results.desc: This set of tools includes a small file, a set of lock picks, a small mirror mounted on a metal handle, a set of narrow-­‐‑bladed scissors, and a pair of pliers. Proficiency with these tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make to disarm traps or open locks.

results.name: Tinderbox
results.desc: This small container holds flint, fire steel, and tinder (usually dry cloth soaked in light oil) used to kindle a fire. Using it to light a torch—or anything else with abundant, exposed fuel—takes an action. Lighting any other fire takes 1 minute.

results.name: Tinker's tools
results.desc:
These special tools include the items needed to pursue a craft or trade.\n\n
Proficiency with a set of artisan’s tools lets you add your proficiency bonus to any ability checks you make using the tools in your craft. Each type of artisan’s tools requires a separate proficiency.

results.name: Torch
results.desc: A torch burns for 1 hour, providing bright light in a 20-­‐‑foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet. If you make a melee attack with a burning torch and hit, it deals 1 fire damage.

results.name: Torpor
results.desc:
A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become poisoned for 4d6 hours. The poisoned creature is incapacitated.
\n\n
**_Ingested._** A creature must swallow an entire dose of ingested poison to suffer its effects. The dose can be delivered in food or a liquid. You may decide that a partial dose has a reduced effect, such as allowing advantage on the saving throw or dealing only half damage on a failed save.
